title: claytonia rubra subsp. depressa
intro: Claytonia rubra subsp. depressa, also known as the western spring beauty or pink purslane, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Portulacaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Native American herbalism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Claytonia rubra subsp. depressa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, making it a versatile remedy for various health conditions.
